output_summary: aligned schemas for datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz; generated 2 schema correspondence groups.
profiled datasets: discogs, lastfm, musicbrainz.
Reused the blocking configuration from state.
Blocking uses `semantic_similarity` for discogs_lastfm, musicbrainz_lastfm, blocking on name, artist; `sorted_neighbourhood` for discogs_musicbrainz, blocking on name.
Performance-wise, pair completeness ranges from 0.90 to 0.96 and candidate counts range from 47615 to 225831.
The config also records dataset id columns.

matcher mode `rule_based` with config source reused from state; evaluated pairs: id_columns, matching_strategies; selected scores: matching_strategies (threshold=0.6, f1=0.896551724137931, threshold=0.7, f1=0.8732394366197184, threshold=0.75); produced 2 pair strategy entries.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline was constructed to load three datasets and perform schema matching using an LLM-based matcher.
Blocking strategies were defined using embedding-based and sorted neighborhood blockers with specific text columns and parameters.
Rule-based matching was applied with string, numeric, and date comparators and defined thresholds for three dataset pairs.
Data fusion was configured with a strategy that assigns attribute fusers: longest_string for six attributes, union for three track-related attributes, and maximum for duration.
Singleton retention was disabled in the fusion engine.

The fusion strategy predominantly uses the longest_string fuser for most attributes, with union and maximum fusers applied to specific fields, indicating a preference for preserving the most complete string values and aggregating track-related fields.
Matching behavior is rule-based with support for string, numeric, and date comparisons, using precomputed blocking candidates and weighted comparators with defined thresholds.
Thresholds were added for the three dataset pairs: 0.6 for discogs-lastfm, 0.7 for discogs-musicbrainz, and 0.75 for musicbrainz-lastfm.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Added extensive schema matching using LLMBasedSchemaMatcher with ChatOpenAI model "gpt-5.1" to align lastfm and musicbrainz schemas to discogs; implemented blocking with EmbeddingBlocker and SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker using semantic similarity and sorted neighborhood methods on specified columns; defined RuleBasedMatcher with detailed comparators and weights for three dataset pairs with thresholds; saved correspondences CSVs.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
Updated matching weights for all three dataset pairs: discogs-lastfm, discogs-musicbrainz, and musicbrainz-lastfm.
Retained the same blocking strategies but updated the matching comparator weights to new values.
Kept the data fusion strategy unchanged with attribute fusers assigned to 10 fields.
Matching behavior shifted by adjusting the weights assigned to comparators in the rule-based matcher for all dataset pairs, increasing emphasis on certain attributes like 'artist' and 'name'.
Fusion behavior remained consistent, continuing to use longest_string as the dominant fuser, with union and maximum as secondary fusers.
Singleton retention in data fusion is disabled (include_singletons set to False).
The pipeline continues to use precomputed optimal blocking strategies with embedding and sorted neighborhood blockers.
The schema matching step uses an LLM-based matcher to align schemas before blocking and matching.
Adjusting matching weights allows fine-tuning of entity matching precision and recall based on attribute importance.
Disabling singleton retention in fusion focuses the output on fused entities with correspondences, likely improving data quality.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 2; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Updated matching configuration weights in pipeline.py for discogs_lastfm to [0.35, 0.4, 0.15, 0.1], discogs_musicbrainz to [0.32, 0.32, 0.16, 0.1, 0.1], musicbrainz_lastfm to [0.32, 0.5, 0.18]; cluster_analysis_result shows many entities with multiple close-scoring matches indicating ambiguous duplicates; health metrics for correspondences_musicbrainz_lastfm include ambiguous_ratio 0.4299, max_cluster_size 21, one_to_many_ratio 0.0886; recommended action is to update matching config.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The pipeline integrates schema matching of lastfm and musicbrainz datasets to discogs using an LLM-based matcher.
Blocking strategies include embedding-based semantic similarity blocking and sorted neighborhood blocking with specified parameters for each dataset pair.
Matching uses rule-based matchers with updated comparators and weights for each dataset pair, with thresholds set at 0.6, 0.7, and 0.75 respectively.
Data fusion strategy applies attribute fusers primarily using longest_string for six attributes, union for three track-related attributes, and maximum for duration.
Singleton records are excluded from the fusion output.
Matching weights were adjusted from initial values to final configuration weights, shifting emphasis among comparators for each dataset pair.
Fusion strategy remains consistent in attribute fuser usage, with no changes in fuser types or thresholds.
The pipeline uses helper functions such as lower_strip for preprocessing string comparisons.
ID columns are consistently defined for each dataset to support blocking and matching.
Output directories are managed by removing existing correspondence directories before saving new results.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 3; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Updated matching weights in pipeline.py to final matching configuration: weights_discogs_lastfm changed from [0.35, 0.4, 0.15, 0.1] to [0.2, 0.25, 0.35, 0.2]; weights_discogs_musicbrainz changed from [0.32, 0.32, 0.16, 0.1, 0.1] to [0.22, 0.22, 0.26, 0.15, 0.15]; weights_musicbrainz_lastfm changed from [0.32, 0.5, 0.18] to [0.4, 0.4, 0.2].
This run was an initial draft of the evaluation adaption.
The dominant evaluation strategy used was a custom DataFusionStrategy named 'music_fusion_strategy' with multiple evaluation functions registered for different columns.

Evaluation functions were grouped by field families such as tokenized_match for text fields (name, artist, release-country, label), numeric_tolerance_match for numeric fields (duration), year_only_match for date fields (release-date), and set_equality_match for set-like fields (tracks_track_name and detected list-like columns).
Added helper functions to parse source IDs and detect gold ID prefixes for alignment.
Implemented logic to build an evaluation view aligning fused data IDs to gold standard IDs.
Added detection and normalization of list-like columns to improve evaluation accuracy.
Included coverage diagnostics to report direct and mapped ID coverage against gold IDs.
Enabled debug output for evaluation results and persisted evaluation metrics to JSON files.
Added XML loading support for the gold test set.
Updated imports and fallback logic to ensure list normalization module is found.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 54.82%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
The main problem in this run is that the data fusion step produces many false positives and false negatives due to the fusion logic.

Specifically, the fusion logic uses a naive union for track-level attributes which causes over-unioning of tracks from different releases or editions within the same cluster, resulting in huge supersets of tracks compared to the gold standard.
Additionally, the fusion logic excludes singleton MusicBrainz records by setting include_singletons=False, causing many gold entities to be missing entirely from the fused output.
The fusion also uses a maximum function for duration, which leads to overestimation when clusters mix standard and extended editions.
There is no source priority or internal cluster consistency check, causing wrong artist assignments and merging of conflicting releases.
The fusion engine treats the merged graph as fully transitive, which causes any chain of reasonably similar releases to be fused together, exacerbating errors.
Track-level attribute accuracies are very low: tracks_track_name_accuracy is 0.087 and tracks_track_duration_accuracy is 0.20.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
The fusion strategy was defined with longest_string fusers for name, artist, release-date, release-country, label, and genre attributes.
Union fusers were applied to tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration attributes.
Maximum fuser was applied to the duration attribute.
Singleton records were excluded from the fusion output by setting include_singletons to False.
Precomputed blocking and matching strategies with specific thresholds were integrated.
The fusion behavior uses a transitive closure approach with union for track-related fields, which leads to over-unioning tracks from multiple editions within clusters.
Duration is fused by taking the maximum value, causing overestimation when clusters mix standard and extended editions.
Singleton records from the gold source (MusicBrainz) are dropped, causing false negatives in fusion.
No in-cluster conflict resolution or sub-clustering is performed, so ambiguous clusters with generic titles and various artists are fused together.
Blocking uses embedding and sorted neighborhood blockers with tuned thresholds for discogs-lastfm, discogs-musicbrainz, and musicbrainz-lastfm pairs.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

Changed evaluation.py imports from load_csv to load_xml; updated test set path to input/datasets/music/testsets/test_set.xml; added nested_handling="aggregate" in load_xml call; replaced fused_eval, fusion_test_set with fused_eval, fusion_test_set_norm after normalization; changed evaluation strategy name to "music_fusion_strategy"; replaced multiple column-specific eval registrations with specific music-related columns and added dynamic registration for detected list-like columns.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy focused on diagnosing fusion errors, especially false positives and false negatives at the fusion stage.
The evaluation used attribute-level accuracy metrics and cluster-level analysis to identify over-fusion and missing fused records.
Evaluation functions included tokenized matching, numeric tolerance matching, year-only matching, and set equality matching for list-like fields.
Track-level attributes showed the most severe errors, with very low accuracy for track name (0.087) and track duration (0.20).
Album-level duration accuracy was moderate (0.739) but affected by missing fused values and overestimation due to fusion of extended editions.
Main attributes such as artist (0.652), release date (0.696), release country (0.696), and name (0.522) had moderate accuracy with some false positives and missing fused values.
Cluster-level analysis revealed ambiguous clusters with one-to-many and hub patterns, especially for generic titles and compilation artists.
Improved fallback logic for evaluation ID assignment when '_id' column is missing by using the DataFrame index.
Refined the detection of the 'list_normalization.py' module path to ensure proper import.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 54.82%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
The main problem in this run is poor accuracy in track-level attributes due to overfusion and ambiguous clusters.
Track attributes such as tracks_track_name and tracks_track_duration have very low accuracy (≈ 0.087 and 0.2 respectively).
There are missing fused values for multiple gold IDs, indicating false negatives at the cluster level.
Scalar attributes like release-country and label suffer from systematic mis-selection due to naive fusion logic.
The fusion logic uses union for list-like attributes and longest_string or maximum for scalar attributes, which is not robust to many-to-many ambiguous correspondences.
tracks_track_name_accuracy is approximately 0.087 (2 out of 23 correct).
tracks_track_duration_accuracy is 0.2 (4 out of 20 correct).
Multiple fused_id = NaN with reason = "missing_fused_value" for tracks_track_name, tracks_track_duration, release-country, and label for several gold IDs (e.g., mbrainz_1834, 25432).
Non-track attributes like release-country, release-date, artist, label, and name have middling accuracy between 0.52 and 0.70.
Cluster analysis shows unhealthy one_to_many_ratio and ambiguous_ratio for discogs_lastfm and discogs_musicbrainz correspondences, causing large ambiguous clusters.
This run represents an initial draft of the pipeline.

The fusion strategy was defined with attribute fusers: 'longest_string' for six scalar attributes including name, artist, release-date, release-country, label, and genre; 'union' for list-like track attributes including tracks_track_name, tracks_track_position, and tracks_track_duration; and 'maximum' for duration.
Singleton retention was disabled by setting include_singletons to False in the fusion engine run.
The fusion strategy applies a union fuser for list-like track attributes, which leads to overfusion and false positives in ambiguous clusters due to permissive matching.
Scalar attributes are fused by selecting the longest string, which can cause systematic mis-selection when clusters contain multiple distinct releases with conflicting values.
No changes were made to matching thresholds or blocking; the fusion logic is the main focus.
The pipeline uses rule-based matching with string, numeric, and date comparators and precomputed blocking strategies.
Schema matching was performed using an LLM-based matcher to align lastfm and musicbrainz schemas to discogs.
The fusion engine runs with debug enabled and outputs debug data in JSON format.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.

evaluation.py updated to improve fallback handling in build_eval_view by checking for presence of "_id" column before assigning eval_id and _eval_cluster_id, added dynamic sys.path insertion for list_normalization.py discovery, modified coverage calculation to handle missing "_id" column gracefully, enhanced comments for evaluation strategy and output persistence.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant evaluation strategy was to analyze error types and causes in the fusion output, focusing on track attributes and cluster-level fusion logic.
The evaluation used set equality matching for list-like attributes and tokenized or numeric tolerance matching for scalar attributes.
Track attributes showed dominant errors with low accuracy in track name (≈0.087) and track duration (0.2), mainly due to overfusion and false positives within clusters.
Scalar attributes such as release-country, label, artist, and release-date had middling accuracy (0.52–0.70) with errors from conflicting values and missing fused values.
The fusion logic used union for list-like attributes and longest_string for scalar attributes, which caused overfusion and mis-selection in ambiguous clusters.
The evaluation included improved ID alignment by building an evaluation view that maps fused rows to gold IDs more accurately.
List-like columns were detected and normalized for evaluation to reduce errors from capitalization and formatting variants.
The evaluation strategy was explicitly defined with appropriate matching functions per attribute.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 54.82%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.

Added extensive new code in pipeline.py implementing schema matching with LLMBasedSchemaMatcher using ChatOpenAI GPT-5.1; blocking with EmbeddingBlocker and SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker on multiple dataset pairs; rule-based matching with RuleBasedMatcher using string, numeric, and date comparators with specified thresholds and weights; saving correspondences to CSV.
